data.title: record-test-status.sh: source file with test-pattern name staged -> executed as test without arguments
data.description:
## Expected Behavior
When a staged source file has a .test-index mapping (e.g., plugins/dso/scripts/test-batched.sh -> tests/scripts/test-batched-state-integrity.sh), record-test-status.sh should run only the MAPPED test files, not execute the source file itself.

## Actual Behavior
record-test-status.sh line 502: fuzzy_is_test_file() matches plugins/dso/scripts/test-batched.sh because its name matches the test-*.sh pattern. The script then runs test-batched.sh directly as a test (without arguments), which prints usage help and exits 1. This false failure blocks commits.

## Reproduction
1. Stage plugins/dso/scripts/test-batched.sh (a source file, not a test file)
2. Run: bash plugins/dso/hooks/record-test-status.sh
3. Observe output: 'Test output for plugins/dso/scripts/test-batched.sh (exit 1)' with usage help text
4. Status recorded as failed despite all mapped tests (test-batched-state-integrity.sh, test-bash-runner-discovery.sh, test-test-batched.sh) passing

## Root Cause
plugins/dso/hooks/record-test-status.sh line 502: fuzzy_is_test_file() uses filename pattern matching (test-*.sh) without distinguishing source files from test files. Source files in plugins/dso/scripts/ that happen to match test naming patterns are incorrectly classified as test files and executed directly.

## Suggested Fix
fuzzy_is_test_file() should also check the file's directory. Files under plugins/dso/scripts/ (or any configured source directory) should not be treated as test files even if their name matches test-*.sh. Alternative: if the file has a .test-index entry as a SOURCE (left side), skip the fuzzy_is_test_file path.
